```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(gt)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
```

```{r load data}
summary_df <- read.delim(here("data/signatures/202506_TB_signatures_info.tsv"), sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
summary_df
```

```{r gt table}
summary_df$technology <- factor(
  summary_df$technology,
  levels = c("microarray", "RNAseq")
)

my_gt_table <- summary_df %>%
  arrange(technology) %>%      # now uses your custom order
  dplyr::select(
    number, dataset_signature_ID, SIGNATURE_NAME, tb_status, source, cell_name, sample_origin, tissue_category, technology,
    up_genes_num, dn_genes_num, control_samples, disease_samples
  ) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "technology") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "TB signature metadata",
    subtitle = "Grouped by technology"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    number = "No.",
    dataset_signature_ID = "Dataset-signature ID",
    SIGNATURE_NAME = "Signature name",
    tb_status = "TB status",
    source = "Source",
    cell_name = "Cell name",
    tissue_category = "Tissue origin",
    sample_origin = "Origin type",
    technology = "Technology",
    up_genes_num = "Upregulated genes",
    dn_genes_num = "Downregulated genes",
    control_samples = "Control samples",
    disease_samples = "Disease samples"
  ) %>%
  fmt_missing(columns = everything(), missing_text = "-") %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = "medium",
    heading.title.font.size = 20,
    heading.subtitle.font.size = 16
  )

# Save the table as HTML
gtsave(my_gt_table, here("figures/tableS1/tableS1_signature_summary_table.html"))

```

```{r check output}
my_gt_table
```
